sentence_reviews.text: Typed multidimensional arrays are the common carrier for parameters, activations, observations, and gradients.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: Chapter 7 'Linear Algebra', especially matrix multiplication, manipulating data matrices, Einstein summation, matrix calculus, and gradients (lines 206-259); AIMA contents.html: Chapter 21.3.2 'Tensor operations in CNNs' and 21.4.1 'Computing gradients in computation graphs' (lines 561-575).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support vectors/matrices, tensor operations, computation graphs, and gradients in ML, but they do not state the exact 'common carrier' wording.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors is to connect Linear algebra, Numerical analysis to downstream capabilities including Images, video & spatial grids, Waveforms, audio & spectrograms, Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: Chapter 7 'Linear Algebra' (lines 206-259), Chapter 14 'Neural Networks for Images' (lines 433-462), and Chapter 21.5 'Spectral clustering' (lines 663-670); AIMA contents.html: Appendix A.2 and Chapter 21.3.2 (lines 768-774 and 561-575).
sentence_reviews.notes: The linear algebra, image, and graph portions are supported as synthesis. Numerical analysis and audio/spectrograms are not directly supported by the inspected passages, so the full connector statement is broader than the evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Images, video & spatial grids
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: Chapter 14 'Neural Networks for Images', including convolutional and pooling layers (lines 433-462); AIMA contents.html: Chapter 21.3 'Convolutional Networks' and 21.3.2 'Tensor operations in CNNs' (lines 561-575).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support image models using tensor operations and linear-algebraic data representations. 'Substrate for' is atlas synthesis rather than exact source wording.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: Chapter 15 'Neural Networks for Sequences', including 1d CNNs for sequence classification and generation (lines 463-478), and Chapter 7 matrix/vector material (lines 206-259).
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports sequence data and vector/matrix representations, but it does not directly mention waveforms, audio, or spectrograms in the inspected locations.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: probabilistic graphical models and spectral clustering / graph Laplacian entries (lines 101-105 and 663-670); AIMA contents.html: relational probability models and causal networks (lines 356-392).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support graph and relational modeling, but the inspected passages do not directly establish tensors as the substrate for hypergraphs and all relational data.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Numerical analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: preprocessing discrete/text data, missing data, dimensionality reduction, matrix operations, and numerical issues in Gaussian processes (lines 15-20, 607-647, 206-259, and 531-539); AIMA contents.html: Appendix A.2 vectors/matrices/linear algebra (lines 768-774).
sentence_reviews.notes: Encoding and representation issues are indirectly supported, and linear algebra is directly supported. Bias, invariance difficulty, and numerical analysis dependency are not directly established by the cited locations.

sentence_reviews.text: Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: Chapter 7 'Linear Algebra' (lines 206-259); AIMA contents.html: Appendix A.2 'Vectors, Matrices, and Linear Algebra' (lines 768-774).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources directly cover vectors, matrices, and linear algebra as mathematical background. The 'rests on capabilities supplied by' wording is atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Numerical analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: numerical issues in Gaussian processes, optimization algorithms, SGD, and computational issues for PCA (lines 531-539, 260-299, and 607-612).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports numerical and computational issues around ML methods, but it does not directly say tensor representations rest on numerical analysis.

edge_reviews.notes: The dependency is strongly supported by sources that present vectors, matrices, matrix operations, and tensor operations as mathematical background for ML.
Numerical methods are relevant to practical tensor computation, but the inspected evidence is indirect and does not establish a core prerequisite as clearly as linear algebra.
